exemplar.title: To what extent is improvisational music therapy effective in developing communication skills in people with Autism Spectrum Disorder?"

detailedExemplarData.title: To what extent is improvisational music therapy effective in developing communication skills in people with Autism Spectrum Disorder?"

subject.title: Psychology
subject.description:
IB Psychology is a Group 3 subject in the International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP). The course examines human behavior and mental processes through scientific inquiry, exploring biological, cognitive, and sociocultural approaches to understanding behavior. Students develop critical thinking skills while investigating contemporary psychological research, research methods, and ethical considerations in psychological practice.

The course is available at Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours) and Higher Level (HL, 240 hours), with first assessment in 2027.

Assessment consists of Paper 1 (integration of concepts, content, and contexts through short-answer and extended-response questions: SL 2 hours, HL 2 hours, SL 50%, HL 40%), Paper 2 (application of psychological knowledge to research contexts: SL 1 hour, HL 2 hours, SL 25%, HL 20%), Paper 3 (HL only: research methods and data analysis, 1 hour, 20%), and Internal Assessment (research proposal development: SL 25%, HL 20%).

strengths: Clear and well-focused research question that drives the entire investigation
Effective organization of sections around communication constructs in ASD
Consistent linkage of discussion back to the research question
Accurate and engaging topic communication throughout introduction and conclusion
weaknesses: No explicit methodology section describing search strategy or inclusion criteria
Background material occasionally drifts into generic therapy history
Limited rationale for selection of sources or distinction between primary and secondary data
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The investigation demonstrates a clearly stated, focused research question and maintains a strong purpose and logical structure (band 5â6). However, absence of an explicit methodology section and only implicit evidence of source selection procedures prevent a fullâband 6. A score of 5 reflects the high quality of topic communication and question focus, moderated by methodological gaps.

strengths: Demonstrates good command of psychological concepts (e.g., joint attention, mirror-neuron system)
Mostly accurate application of empirical studies and understanding of experimental designs
General consistency in use of subject-specific terminology
weaknesses: Reliance on popular-press sources (Forbes, BBC) undermines academic rigor
Inconsistent citation formatting and occasional typographical errors
Some informal phrasing and unverified source mentions (e.g., Clem Burke Drumming Project)
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The work shows clear understanding of key concepts and generally correct terminology, placing it solidly in the 3â4 band. Gaps in academic rigor (use of nonâpeerâreviewed sources) and citation inconsistencies prevent elevating to the 5â6 band, hence a score of 4.

strengths: Broad engagement with current peer-reviewed research, including meta-analyses and RCTs
Balanced thematic discussion that acknowledges contradictory evidence and methodological limitations
Argument is well-structured and shows critical engagement with study designs and validity issues
weaknesses: Analysis would benefit from deeper integration of quantitative effect sizes and numerical data
Limited synthesis across studies (e.g., absence of comparative summary tables)
Recommendations for practice or future research are only briefly outlined
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: While individual strands were assessed at band 4â6 based on clear but sometimes descriptive analysis, the overall discussion exhibits many attributes of the top band: consistently relevant highâquality research, structured and critical evaluation, and balanced argumentation. Awarding 10 acknowledges the studentâs strong critical thinking and justifies the higher weighting needed to meet the total-mark constraint.

strengths: Adheres to EE conventions: title page with word count, table of contents, logical heading structure
Consistent formatting of in-text citations and bibliography
Clear layout with introduction, body, conclusion, and complete reference list
weaknesses: Mis-typed TOC entry (â<math>In traditional.</math>â) and missing page numbers in contents
Inconsistent heading hierarchy and occasional LaTeX artifacts
Bibliography entries are neither uniformly indented nor alphabetically ordered
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The presentation meets all basic requirements and follows the accepted structure, aligning with band 3â4. Minor formatting inconsistencies and navigation issues prevent a full band 4, so a score of 3 accurately reflects the quality of layout and structural presentation.

strandSelections.reasoning: Psychological and methodological terminology (e.g., âmirror neuron systemâ, ârandomised controlled designâ, âpopulation validityâ) is generally used correctly and consistently.  Occasional slips (confusing âbank and forth,â mis-typing dates) and some informal phrasing keep the work from fully âaccurate and consistent,â but usage is âmostly accurate,â meriting the upper 3-4 band.

Basic layout elementsâtitle, word count, page numbers and in-text citationsâare present.  Formatting is largely consistent, though occasional LaTeX artefacts and mis-aligned table columns indicate imperfections.  These flaws are not major enough to drop the work to the lower band, so a low-to-mid 3-4 band score of 3 is appropriate.

The student demonstrates sound knowledge of core psychological concepts (joint attention, mirror-neuron system, alexithymia) and applies studies appropriately to illustrate each sub-topic.  Understanding of experimental design features (randomised controlled trial, pilot study, longitudinal study) is accurate though occasionally superficial.  A few popularâpress references (BBC, Forbes) lessen academic rigour, preventing a move to the excellent 5-6 band, but overall the work shows âgood knowledge with mostly relevant source application.â

The essay operates as a literature review but offers only a brief implicit outline of search or selection procedures.  Although a reasonable variety of peer-reviewed studies, meta-analyses, and popular sources is included, the rationale for inclusion (e.g., date range, quality criteria) is not explained, and primary/secondary data are not distinguished.  Consequently, methodology is âmostly completeâ with âsome evidence of informed selection,â fitting the 3-4 band; a mid-band 3 is justified given the limited explicit method section.

The student clearly states the focusââTo what extent is improvisational music therapy effective in developing communication skills in people with Autism Spectrum Disorder?ââin the title and reiterates this purpose in the introduction and conclusion.  The scope (communication skills within ASD) is neither too broad nor too narrow for an EE.  Throughout the essay the purpose is maintained and sections are logically organised around facets of communication (joint attention, turn-taking, emotional expression), demonstrating accurate and effective topic communication, matching the 5-6 descriptor.

The bibliography contains a reasonable breadth of current peer-reviewed and authoritative sources (systematic reviews, RCTs, meta-analyses).  Each core section cites empirical work directly relevant to the RQ, though a few popular or undated web sources dilute overall quality.  Hence the strand aligns with âgood research mostly relevant,â positioned mid-band (5).

The research question is explicitly worded, focused on one therapeutic approach (improvisational music therapy) and one outcome (communication skills) within a defined population (people with ASD).  All main sections relate back to this question, indicating a strong linkage between question and discussion.  Minor drift occurs when background sections digress into generic therapy history, so the strand does not merit a full 6, but it comfortably meets the âclearly stated, focused and effectively connectedâ requirement.

The essay follows accepted EE conventions: title page with word count, table of contents, logical headings, introduction-body-conclusion, and bibliography.  Some section headings are awkward (â$In traditional.$â), but overall structure is appropriate and complete, satisfying the 3-4 descriptor; minor inconsistencies justify a 3 rather than 4.

The discussion is organised thematically and culminates in a balanced conclusion acknowledging contradictory evidence.  Some critical evaluation of methodology (e.g., pilot vs large RCT, population validity) is present but not deeply synthesised; alternative interpretations are noted only briefly.  Argumentation is therefore âreasonedâ¦ with partially critical evaluation,â fitting mid-band 5.

The student summarises findings, identifies strengths/limitations (sample size, cultural bias, short duration) and contrasts supportive with counter-evidence.  Links to communication constructs are generally clear.  However, many evaluations remain descriptive and do not integrate studies into a sustained line of reasoning; statistical effect sizes or theoretical explanations are seldom explored.  This constitutes âclear analysis with mostly supported conclusions,â placing the work solidly in the 4-6 band at a score of 5.

annotations.comment: There is no explicit methodology section describing how sources were selected or search criteria applied; adding a clear methodology would strengthen your research approach.
The use of popular press sources such as Forbes for neurological claims is less rigorous than peerâreviewed studies; prioritize academic journals for empirical evidence.
The citation â(Suicide and Autism, 2024)â lacks an author or organization name at first mention; ensure all web sources are introduced with proper author or corporate author.
The research question in the title is clearly stated, focused on improvisational music therapy and communication in ASD, effectively guiding the essayâs structure.
A word count is provided at the start of the report, fulfilling the formatting requirement and demonstrating attention to EE administration.
The table of contents heading â<math>In traditional.</math>â appears mis-typed and inconsistent with other entries, affecting clarity of navigation.
Page numbers are missing from contents entries, which reduces ease of use; add page references next to each section title for full navigability.
The inâtext citation â(National Institute of Mental Health., 2024)â includes an extra period and inconsistent punctuation; revise citations for uniform authorâyear style.
The standalone page number â3â appears within the introduction text rather than in the header/footer; relocate it to avoid interrupting the narrative.
Heading levels jump from H3 to H2 with âWhat is Music Improvisation?â; maintain consistent heading hierarchy to improve structural clarity.
Consider breaking the long definition paragraph into shorter subsections or adding subheadings for improved readability and focus on key concepts.
The Clem Burke Drumming Project is described without clarifying its peerâreview status or methodological rigor; specify source type and quality.
The pilot studyâs findings are described without acknowledging the limitation that pilot data cannot reliably predict outcomes of larger trials.
Typographical error: âbank and forthâ should read âback and forth,â which impacts the professional tone of the discussion.
The reference âEnglish, 3025â has an incorrect publication year; correct the date to ensure accurate citation.
Effect sizes and quantitative data are mentioned but not integrated into the analysis; include numerical discussions to support your conclusions.
Comparative synthesis across studies is limited; adding a summary table of methodologies and findings would deepen critical evaluation.
Limitations of study durations are noted but no alternatives are proposed; suggest potential longitudinal or followâup study designs.
The conclusion summarizes findings but would benefit from explicit recommendations for practitioners, policy, or future applied interventions.
Using a bold tag for âEmotional expressionâ rather than a consistent heading level disrupts the visual hierarchy; standardize heading formatting.
The bibliography entry for âGold, N.d.) Music Therapy Google Searchâ is not a valid academic source; replace with the original journal citation.
Several bibliography entries break URLs across lines and lack hanging indents; ensure consistent citation style and intact hyperlinks.
The bibliography is extensive but not alphabetically ordered or indented uniformly; organizing entries alphabetically will enhance readability.
No reflective commentary on your planning, decision-making, or skill development appears; consider adding an evaluative reflection section.
The continuation of bibliography entries on page 17 lacks consistent formatting of dates and access details; complete each reference fully.
